{{Quote|Sariss! Prepare the ''Vengeance'' for hyperspace.|[[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]]|<ref name="JK:DFII" />}}
The '''''Vengeance''''' was a slender, black [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] that served as the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] in the years before and after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="TDFS1" /> It was commanded by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Sysco]].<ref name="DF:RA" />

==Characteristics==
===Dimensions===
The ''Vengeance'' was based on the standard Super Star Destroyer design, the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class]].<ref name="ROTFN" /> As such, the ''Vengeance'' was approximately 19 kilometers long,<ref name="SOTG07" /> and was named after the Dark Jedi's old [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], which he commanded some time prior to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].<ref name="DF:SFTE">''[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire]]''</ref> In addition, because of its dimensions, it sometimes possessed the standard classification of a [[Space station/Legends|space station]]<ref name="SOTG07" />

The most striking feature of the ship was its slender frame, which was more narrow than standard Star Destroyers, almost sword-like in comparison to the usual dagger shape.<ref name="JK:DFII" />

The ''Vengeance''{{'s}} height contrasted its length, which was little more than 150 meters and with downward sloping armor dominating most of the dorsal frame. The ship's bridge was extensive, but also covered much of the command tower, unlike most other [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] warships.<ref name="JK:DFII" /> It is known that it was one of the larger Super Star Destroyers of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial era]], and its length (exceeding 5,000 meters) put it firmly in the [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]] category.<ref name="TEGtW" />

===Offensive and defensive systems===
The ''Vengeance'' was only shown defeating a small, probably unmanned [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]],<ref name="JK:DFII" /> but it had armaments approaching Star Dreadnoughts of the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class]].<ref name="SOTG07" />

===Propulsion systems===
[[File:Vengeancestern.jpg|thumb|left|180px|The ''Vengeance''{{'s}} array of engines.]]
The ''Vengeance'' had a cluster of engines, consisting of three primary engines, two secondary engines flanking the vessel and six auxiliary engines clustering around the primary ones.<ref name="JK:DFII" />

===Complement===
[[File:Jerec.jpg|thumb|180px|Jerec and Yun in the docking bay of the ''Vengeance'' with an array of TIEs.]]
Little is known about the capabilities of the ''Vengeance'', except that it carried a number of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] and [[Assault shuttle/Legends|assault shuttles]].<ref name="DF:RA" />

===Command bridge===
[[File:Vengeance_command_tower.jpg|thumb|left|180px|The ''Vengeance''{{'s}} command tower.]]
The ''Vengeance'' was unique in that it lacks the standard Kuat Drive Yards-style wedge shaped command bridge tower. Instead, the command bridge is located on a small square ridge near the stern of the ship.<ref name="JK:DFII" />

==History==
[[File:Vengeance.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The ''Vengeance''{{'s}} with ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers.]]
To accommodate Jerec's increasingly large ego, he had a personal warship built to be his new command ship. The vessel began construction some time before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], as Jerec donated his ''[[Vengeance II]]'' to [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Kendal Ozzel/Legends|Kendal Ozzel's]] [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron]] while waiting for the larger vessel to be finished.<ref name="TDFS1" />
Jerec used the ''Vengeance'' as a meeting place for him and his [[Seven Dark Jedi]] in the following years.<ref name="TDFS2" /> The ship also served as flagship for the [[Vengeance Battle Group]], an Imperial military formation which aided Jerec in his search for the [[Valley of the Jedi]].<ref name="DF:JK" />

Some time after the Battle of Endor, Jerec used it to travel to the planet [[Dorlo]]. There, he sent down a task force led by his Dark Jedi underlings to [[Mission to Dorlo|find and capture]] the fugitive [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], [[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]]. From him, Jerec learned that the secret to the Valley of the Jedi was in the [[Katarn family compound|home]] of [[Morgan Katarn]] on [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]], a man he had executed, from a world he had [[Subjugation of Sulon|subjugated]] years ago.<ref name="DF:RA" /><ref name="JK:DFII" />

[[File:holomaptoRuusan.jpg|thumb|Jerec receives the digital map to Ruusan from 8t88.]]
The ''Vengeance'' brought Jerec's forces to Sulon, and minions discovered and salvaged the pieces of the [[Map to the Valley of the Jedi]] created by Morgan Katarn. His collaborator, the [[information broker/Legends|information broker]] [[droid/Legends|droid]] [[8t88/Legends|8t88]] was stationed in his former palace, the [[Government House (Sulon)|Government House]] where he managed to reconstruct the map, make a digital 3d copy and transfer it to Jerec on board the ''Vengeance'' with a [[hologram/Legends|holographic]] projection. With the [[Holomap/Legends|holomap]] to the lost planet [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] known to him, he ordered [[Sariss]] to prepare the ship for [[hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]].<ref name="DF:RA" />

The ship was last seen orbiting [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]], the location of the Valley of the Jedi. The [[Battle group/Legends|battle group]] it led seemed to represent a relatively common force composition, judging from the reaction of [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] operative [[Alfonso Luiz Obota]], who observed the group during the [[Mission to Ruusan]] in 5 ABY.<ref name="DF:JK" />

[[File:Byss_fleet2.jpg|thumb|Several sword-shaped vessels in orbit of [[Byss/Legends|Byss]] towards the top left and right.]]

Three [[Vengeance-class dreadnought|''Vengeance''-class dreadnoughts]], mass-produced versions of Jerec's flagship, participated in the retaking of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] and the subsequent [[Imperial Civil War]].<ref name="TEGtW" /> In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> various ''Vengeance''-class dreadnoughts were seen near [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], during [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor Palpatine's]] [[Operation Shadow Hand]] campaign.<ref name="DE4">[[Dark Empire 4|''Dark Empire'' 4]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
In ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', there was a mention of the [[Vengeance-class dreadnought|''Vengeance''-class dreadnought]] as well as the [[Javelin (Vengeance-class)|''Javelin'']]. Although artwork of it was never shown, the name of the ship class suggested a connection to Jerec's flagship of the same name. A post made by Paul Urquhart on the ''The Essential Guide to Warfare'' forum for TheForce.Net revealed that the similarities and connections between the ship class and Jerec's ship was intentional.<ref name="McEwok" />
